 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1h8z","title":"P3.4: Search UI Component","description":"# Search UI Component

**Parent Phase:** Phase 3: Web Viewer
**Depends On:** P3.3 (sqlite-wasm Integration)
**Duration:** 2-3 days

## Goal

Build the search interface with query input, agent/time/workspace filters, and virtualized result list.

## Technical Approach

### search.js Module

Main responsibilities:
- Query input with debounce
- Agent/time/workspace filter dropdowns
- FTS5 query routing (natural language vs code search)
- Virtual scrolling for large result sets
- Click handling to open conversations

### FTS5 Query Strategy

Two indexes serve different search patterns:
1. messages_fts (porter stemmer): English prose
2. messages_code_fts (unicode61): snake_case, paths

Auto-detect based on query pattern (/[_.]|[a-z][A-Z]/).

### FTS5 Query Escaping (Critical)

Wrap terms in double-quotes to prevent injection:
"term1" "term2" instead of raw input.

### Virtual List for Performance

For 10K+ results, only render visible items:
- Track scroll position
- Calculate visible range
- Render buffer items above/below
- Transform content position

### Snippet Display

Use FTS5 snippet() function for context:
snippet(table, 0, "<mark>", "</mark>", "...", 64)

## Test Cases

1. Empty search shows recent conversations
2. Text search returns FTS5 results
3. Agent filter limits results
4. Time filter limits results
5. Code patterns route to code FTS
6. FTS5 special chars escaped
7. Virtual scroll handles 10K+ results

## Files to Create

- src/pages_assets/search.js
- src/pages_assets/fts-utils.js
- src/pages_assets/virtual-list.js

## Exit Criteria

1. Search input works
2. Filters work independently and combined
3. Results render with snippets
4. Virtual scrolling smooth for large sets
5. FTS5 queries do not throw errors
6. Click on result opens conversation","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2026-01-07T01:36:49.393573897Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T16:05:24.396263835Z","closed_at":"2026-01-12T16:05:24.396263835Z","close_reason":"P3.4 Search UI implemented: search.js with query input, agent/time filters, FTS5 escaping, result cards, keyboard nav, relative time display.","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1h8z","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-fxaw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T01:36:57.874576947Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1hk","title":"Phase 6: Workspace Path Rewriting (Optional Enhancement)","description":"# Phase 6: Workspace Path Rewriting (Optional Enhancement)

## Overview
When sessions are synced from remote machines, the workspace paths in conversations
refer to the remote filesystem (e.g., /home/user/projects/foo on remote, but
/Users/me/projects/foo on local). This phase adds optional path rewriting rules
so that file references in search results point to valid local paths.

## Goals
1. Define path mapping rules in source configuration
2. Apply mappings when displaying results (not when indexing)
3. Provide fallback behavior when mapping unavailable

## Example Configuration
```toml
[[sources]]
name = "laptop"
host = "user@laptop.local"
[sources.path_mappings]
"/home/user/projects" = "/Users/me/projects"
"/opt/work" = "/Volumes/Work"
```

## Technical Considerations
- Mappings applied at display time, not storage (preserve original data)
- Multiple mappings may match; use longest prefix match
- Unmapped paths displayed as-is with visual indicator

## Why Optional
This feature is complex and may not be needed by all users. Some users may prefer
to see original paths. Making it opt-in reduces confusion.

## Dependencies
- Requires Phase 5 completion (sources config exists)
- Could be deferred to post-MVP

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Path mappings configurable per source
- [ ] Mappings applied to displayed workspace paths
- [ ] Unmapped paths clearly indicated
- [ ] Original paths preserved in storage
- [ ] Documentation explains mapping syntax","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"","created_at":"2025-12-16T06:01:13.943175Z","updated_at":"2026-01-02T13:44:58.374804662Z","closed_at":"2025-12-17T07:49:20.154147Z","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1hk","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-bgi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T06:01:49.364373Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1hk0","title":"Fix test compilation errors (crate name and field name)","description":"Two test files fail to compile:

Both are simple typos that prevent test compilation.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:47:54.023988083Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T04:51:12.017038192Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T04:51:12.016970656Z","close_reason":"Fixed test compilation: 1) crypto_vectors.rs: fixed field name v.okm→v.expected_okm, added hkdf_extract_expand import; 2) reproduce_query_bug.rs: fixed crate name cass→coding_agent_search, replaced Default::default() with explicit fields, marked as #[ignore] since it's a bug reproduction test","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1ihb","title":"Task 8: Final Validation and User Signoff","description":"# Objective
Export this current session to HTML and validate against requirements, then get user signoff.

## Validation Checklist

### Structural Validation
- [ ] Tool calls appear as icons in message header (NOT separate bubbles)
- [ ] Hover on tool badge shows popover with details
- [ ] ONE article element per assistant message (not per tool call)
- [ ] Tool results consolidated into parent message

### Visual Validation
- [ ] Glassmorphism effect visible (semi-transparent cards with blur)
- [ ] Ambient gradient background present
- [ ] Glow effects on hover
- [ ] Color scheme matches Terminal Noir (deep space palette)
- [ ] Tool badges are tiny and unobtrusive
- [ ] Tool badges have proper status colors (green/red/amber)

### Responsive
- [ ] Desktop: Wide layout, comfortable spacing
- [ ] Mobile: Toolbar at bottom, larger touch targets

### Themes
- [ ] Dark theme looks premium
- [ ] Light theme looks premium
- [ ] Theme toggle works

### Code Quality
- [ ] cargo check passes
- [ ] cargo clippy passes
- [ ] cargo fmt --check passes
- [ ] All unit tests pass
- [ ] All E2E tests pass

## User Signoff
Present the exported HTML to user and ask:
"Does this meet your expectations for:
1. Tool call consolidation (icons in header)?
2. Visual design (glassmorphism, glow, colors)?
3. Mobile/desktop experience?
4. Light/dark themes?"

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All checklist items verified
- [ ] User confirms satisfaction with result
- [ ] Any feedback addressed","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T21:59:23.793705644Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T21:59:34.736998260Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1ihb","depends_on_id":"2jxn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T21:59:23.801532623Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1ihb","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2ebr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T21:59:34.736920746Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mv","title":"P2.2 Indexer multi-root orchestration","description":"# Indexer Multi-Root Orchestration

## Context
The indexer needs to build a list of scan roots and orchestrate scanning across them, injecting provenance appropriately.

## Location
src/indexer/mod.rs

## Current Flow
1. Build single ScanContext with data_dir
2. For each connector, call scan()
3. Persist results

## New Flow
1. Build list of scan roots:
   - Local roots (from watch_roots() or equivalent)
   - Remote mirror roots (from sources table)
2. Group roots by connector affinity
3. For each connector:
   - For each relevant root:
     - Create ScanContext with that root
     - Call scan()
     - Inject provenance into results
     - Persist with source_id

## Implementation

### Build Scan Roots List
\`\`\`rust
fn build_scan_roots(storage: &SqliteStorage) -> Vec<ScanRoot> {
    let mut roots = Vec::new();
    
    // Add local roots (existing logic from watch_roots())
    for local_root in local_default_roots() {
        roots.push(ScanRoot {
            path: local_root,
            origin: Origin::local(),
            platform: Some(Platform::current()),
            workspace_rewrites: vec![],
        });
    }
    
    // Add remote mirror roots
    for source in storage.list_sources()? {
        if source.kind == SourceKind::Ssh {
            let mirror_root = data_dir.join("remotes").join(&source.id).join("mirror");
            if mirror_root.exists() {
                roots.push(ScanRoot {
                    path: mirror_root,
                    origin: Origin {
                        source_id: source.id.clone(),
                        kind: source.kind,
                        host: source.host_label.clone(),
                    },
                    platform: source.platform,
                    workspace_rewrites: parse_rewrites(&source.config),
                });
            }
        }
    }
    
    roots
}
\`\`\`

### Connector Affinity
Not all roots apply to all connectors. For example:
- ClaudeCodeConnector cares about ~/.claude or remotes/*/mirror/**/.claude
- AiderConnector cares about .aider.chat.history.md anywhere

For now: let connectors handle root relevance via their detect() logic.

### Provenance Injection
After connector.scan() returns:
\`\`\`rust
for conv in conversations.iter_mut() {
    conv.metadata["cass"] = json!({
        "origin": {
            "source_id": scan_root.origin.source_id,
            "kind": scan_root.origin.kind.as_str(),
            "host": scan_root.origin.host,
        }
    });
}
\`\`\`

## Dependencies
- P2.1 (ScanContext extended)
- P1.3 (storage accepts source_id)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Indexer builds list of scan roots
- [ ] Local roots work as before
- [ ] Provenance injected into metadata
- [ ] Results persisted with correct source_id
- [ ] Tests verify multi-root behavior","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2025-12-16T05:57:31.805169Z","updated_at":"2025-12-16T16:33:11.166283Z","closed_at":"2025-12-16T16:33:11.166283Z","close_reason":"Implemented multi-root orchestration with build_scan_roots(), inject_provenance(), and provenance extraction in persist::map_to_internal(). All 294 tests pass.","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mv","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1v7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T05:58:17.128097Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
